changes_applied.fix: Rewrite description with concrete scenarios + triggers
changes_applied.details: New description lists specific frameworks (React, Vue, Next.js, Django, Rails, static), scenarios (validate the site, take screenshots, check console, responsive testing), and distinguishes this from 'just builds'. Added 2 new YAML triggers.

changes_applied.fix: Fix port hardcoding — use detected $PORT throughout
changes_applied.details: Critical bug: Step 1 detected framework but Step 2 hardcoded port 3000, breaking Django (8000), Flask (5000), Vite (5173). Step 1 now tails dev server log to extract the actual port via regex and exports it as $PORT. All subsequent steps (health check, route coverage) use $PORT.

changes_applied.fix: Add tool-choice guidance (Playwright MCP vs Chrome DevTools MCP)
changes_applied.details: Added 'Choosing your browser tool' table at top, explaining when each MCP is best (Playwright: cross-browser, CI, mature; Chrome DevTools: real Chrome debugging, live DevTools features). Answers the audit's complaint that users had to guess which tool to use.

Reordered detection (pnpm > yarn > npm to prefer faster tools), added fallback guidance: if detection fails or the 'dev' script is named differently (start/dev:local/serve/develop), open package.json and check the scripts section.

changes_applied.fix: Explain how to discover form validation rules before testing
changes_applied.details: Added preamble to Step 6: inspect HTML attributes (required, minlength, pattern), trigger a bad submit to read error messages, or check API schema (OpenAPI, Django serializer, Zod). Warns that generic test values like 'SecurePass123!' may not pass stricter rules.

changes_applied.fix: Replace hardcoded routes array with source-of-truth extraction
changes_applied.details: Step 8 now tells the reader to pull routes from the framework's routing definition (React Router files, Django show_urls, Rails bin/rails routes, sitemap.xml, OpenAPI spec) rather than hand-maintain a bash array.

changes_skipped.reason: Valuable but substantial — a working harness needs framework detection, port discovery, step orchestration, error handling, and cleanup. Better as a subagent-produced artifact when usage allows.

description_before: Web validation via browser automation: health checks, screenshots at 375/768/1920px, form testing, console/network validation. Detects CORS, hydration, CSS issues.
description_after: Use whenever validating a web app (React, Vue, Next.js, Django, Rails, static site) end-to-end through a real browser — not just 'it builds' or 'dev server starts'. Covers health check, screenshot-based navigation, console error detection, network request inspection, form submission, responsive layout at mobile/tablet/desktop widths, and route coverage. Reach for it on phrases like 'validate the site', 'take screenshots', 'check the console', 'responsive testing', 'does the page load', or when a frontend change needs proof that real users won't see errors.
self_review_notes: The port-hardcoding bug was the biggest functional problem — whoever ran this on Django/Flask would have failed silently and blamed the app. Now fixed. Tool-choice table eliminates the 'which MCP?' guessing. Form validation and route coverage sections now teach how to do the work correctly instead of providing unrealistic templates.
